data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T154343-ae2fdd7f/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T154343-ae2fdd7f/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T154343-ae2fdd7f/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T154343-ae2fdd7f/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T154343-ae2fdd7f/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-259bb4e2">
    <title>agent: cleanly unwind interrupted work and try attempts</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
After the root context is cancelled by Ctrl-C/SIGTERM, `ddx work` and `ddx try` must finish the TD-031 interruption path: preserve available evidence, classify the attempt as mechanically disrupted, release any non-terminal bead claim, remove runtime liveness state, and leave the unfinished bead re-claimable. The current code has partial context-cancellation classification but needs explicit end-to-end coverage for the CLI work/try paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:859-878 marks non-success reports as disrupted when ctx is cancelled, but the surrounding cleanup/unclaim behavior is only incidentally covered by generic failure branches.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:812-846 starts and stops claim heartbeat around an attempt; interrupted attempts must stop liveness and release or clear claims deterministically.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:797-807 creates a dispatch context and only the bead-extra cancel path forces `operator_cancel`; plain context cancellation should remain `signal`/mechanical disruption, not `operator_cancel`.
- cli/cmd/try.go:333-342 and cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1921-1940 propagate `cmd.Context()` into worker.Run but need regression tests that cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and reports the signal path.
- docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-031-bead-state-machine.md:289-314 defines the claim release and no-cooldown requirements for worker shutdown/interruption.

PROPOSED FIX
- Make interrupted attempts produce a stable disrupted/interrupted report reason (for example `context_canceled` / `signal`) without counting as model no-progress.
- Ensure the loop unclaims interrupted non-terminal beads and removes any external claim heartbeat/liveness record.
- Ensure interrupted attempts do not set `execute-loop-retry-after` unless a separate retryable condition was already recorded before cancellation.
- Ensure `ddx try <id>` reports a clear interrupted outcome and exits non-zero after cleanup.
- Add regression tests around worker.Run plus command-level try/work cancellation paths using controllable fake executors/runners.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not preserve worktrees as resumable by default. Cleanup/retry-from-scratch is the reliable default.
- Do not alter operator cancel-marker semantics (`cancel-requested` / `operator_cancel`).
- Do not change successful landing/review behavior when cancellation arrives after a terminal mutation.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestWorkInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsBead verifies cancelling the work command context during an in-flight attempt releases the claim and leaves the bead open/re-claimable.
2. TestWorkInterrupt_DoesNotSetNoProgressCooldown verifies interrupted work does not set `execute-loop-retry-after` or increment no-progress suppression.
3. TestWorkInterrupt_RemovesClaimLiveness verifies external claim heartbeat/liveness is removed or stale-safe after cleanup.
4. TestTryInterrupt_InFlightAttemptUnclaimsTarget verifies `ddx try <id>` cancellation leaves the target bead re-claimable and exits non-zero with an interrupted/signal-class outcome.
5. TestTryInterrupt_DoesNotReportOperatorCancel verifies plain context cancellation is not classified as `operator_cancel` unless the bead cancel marker was present.
6. TestInterruptedAfterTerminalMutation_DoesNotUndoClose verifies cancellation after a successful terminal mutation does not reopen or unclaim a closed bead.
7. `rg -n "context.Canceled|context_canceled|signal|operator_cancel|execute-loop-retry-after" cli/internal/agent cli/cmd` shows cancellation paths are explicit and comments distinguish signal interruption from operator cancel.
8. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run "TestWorkInterrupt|TestTryInterrupt|TestInterrupted|TestCancel|TestClaim" -count=1` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:agent, area:beads, area:cli, kind:bug, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:TD-031, interrupt</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-99a3be32" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="f4563c74a101707e1538414fb3a4ee4fea7f45bd" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T154343-ae2fdd7f"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

Committed as `e2ca60343` with message `fix: handle interrupted work and try cancellation [ddx-259bb4e2]`.
